Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Nov-24-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JOSHUA FALAKASSA, Esq. of Bokhour Law Group, P.C. (Tel: 818-456-6168) for the plaintiff Anthony Williams;
Atty. JOHN BOGGS, Esq. of Fine, Boggs & Perkins LLP (Tel: 415-378-3150) for defendants Sonic Automotive, Inc., FAA Concord H, INC.; and Firstamerica Automotive, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS SONIC AUTOMOTIVE, INC., FIRST AMERICA AUTOMOTIVE, INC., AND FAA CONCORD H, INC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' petition to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is denied on the ground that the arbitration agreement is procedurally and substantively unconscionable. In OTO, L.L.C. v. Kho (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111 and Davis v. TWC Dealer Group, Inc. (2019) 41 Cal.App.5th 662, our Supreme Court and the Court of Appeal found substantially identical arbitration agreements utilized by automobile dealerships to be unconscionable. Defendants' attempts to distinguish those cases on their facts are unconvincing. Further, their criticism of the Court of Appeal's decision in Davis for "coarsely applying OTO to a non-Berman matter," "misapply[ing] the OTO decision" and "ignoring" its language and reasoning (Memo. at 14:5-6, 14:20-23; Reploy at 7:26) is entirely improper. That decision is binding on this Court. (Auto Equity Sales, Inc. v. Superior Court (1962) 57 Cal.2d 450, 455.) Remarkably, although both the agreement and the process at issue here are nearly indistinguishable from those in Davis (in which the plaintiff also was required to sign three different agreements), Defendants offensively (and baselessly) accuse Plaintiff of "throwing any and all arguments against the wall to see what sticks in an attempt to avoid his obligation to submit his claim to binding arbitration." (Reply at 1:9-11.) Defendants' counsel does not appear to have learned anything from the Court of Appeal's reproval. (See Davis, 41 Cal.App.5th at 665 [indicating that the court published its opinion "to also affirm-and remind the profession of-the importance of candor toward the court"]; id. at 676-678 ["closing observations" regarding counsel's violation of duty of candor].)

The Court to issue a written order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Carlito Mandia, CSR No. 9689, cmandia@sftc.org (302/EPS)
